fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Virginia Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity virginia, how to transfer audiologist license to virginia, audiologist compact virginia, virginia audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.mailing_address: Virginia Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology, Perimeter Center, 9960 Mayland Drive, Suite 300, Henrico, VA 23233-1463

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Virginia.

steps.title: Review Licensure Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the educational (doctoral degree in audiology) and examination (e.g., PRAXIS exam) requirements set by the Virginia Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the DHP Online Services System, then log in and complete the Audiologist application. Pay the initial application fee of $130 during this step.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, confirming your doctoral degree in audiology, to be sent directly from your accredited institution to the Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Submit National Exam Scores
steps.description: Arrange for official scores from the PRAXIS exam in audiology (or other board-approved national examination) to be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within the DHP Online Services System for updates or requests for additional information from the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Virginia audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Virginia have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Virginia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Virginia without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Virginia
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Virginia, you must register and submit your application online through the DHP Online Services System.

official_links.registration_steps: Click 'Register a Person' and follow the prompts to create an account. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License' to start the Audiologist application.
